title: hieracium murorum subsp. chlorophanifolium
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. chlorophanifolium, also known as a subspecies of the rock hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow or white daisy-like flowers and edible leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. chlorophanifolium include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw in salads or as a cooked green.
